screening_method: Read current repo docs and calibration_seed index.
Inspected local public clones under .tmp/g6d4_meta with git diff metadata.
Applied ADR-75 dependency policy before any partition freeze.
Asked cgpro for the exact-four versus partial-freeze decision.
Did not run post-freeze scoped pytest because no freeze was performed.

candidate_actions.reason: Clean dependency path via setup.py test extras, changed tests, and a narrow table/view API claim with scoped pytest node tests/test_create.py::test_bad_table_and_view_exceptions. Not frozen because the tranche has only two clean candidates.
candidate_actions.dependency_screen: setup.py extras_require includes test dependencies; no requirements-file rescue needed.
candidate_actions.candidate_claim: Database.table(name) rejects views and Database.view(name) rejects non-view names while __getitem__ still dispatches view names.

candidate_actions.reason: Clean dependency path via PEP 735 tests group, changed test, and a narrow ConsoleRenderer.force_colors property claim with scoped pytest node tests/test_dev.py::TestConsoleRendererForceColorsProperty::test_toggle_force_colors_updates_styles_and_levels. Not frozen because the tranche has only two clean candidates.
candidate_actions.dependency_screen: pyproject.toml dependency-groups tests includes pytest; no requirements-file rescue needed.
candidate_actions.candidate_claim: ConsoleRenderer.force_colors is mutable and updates _force_colors, styles, level styles, and columns.

candidate_actions.reason: Dependency path is likely clean, but the candidate overlaps an already pinned --functions behavior case and comes from a broad PR/diff. It cannot be used merely to fill an exact-four tranche.
Dependency path is modern, but the diff is broad release/version/typing maintenance rather than a narrow behavioral claim.
ADR-73/ADR-75 requirements-file/tox-only dependency boundary; not eligible for G-6d.4 without a separate pre-selection ADR.
Broad pytest-warning/resource cleanup surface over many files, not a narrow claim/test-scope candidate.
Mostly test infrastructure and SQLite support metadata; weaker user-visible claim fit than the accepted possible future candidates.
Relevant tests depend on optional better-exceptions availability outside the standard tests group.
Test ordering/infrastructure change rather than a narrow target behavior claim.
Test helper migration / pretend dependency cleanup, not a narrow behavior claim.
Broad modernization/cleanup surface across many runtime and test files.
Refactor-only terminal initialization change with no changed runnable test scope.
Cleanup/refactor with no changed runnable test file.
Public base/head boundary was not verifiable against the indexed metadata during local screening.
Non-Python or Rust-adapter surface conflicts with the current Python-first scope.

sources.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
sources.why_it_matters: Repo-level SWE requires multi-file context and execution-grounded evaluation.

sources.title: Agentless: Demystifying LLM-based Software Engineering Agents
sources.why_it_matters: Structured localization, repair, and validation can outperform over-complex agents.

sources.title: Why SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities
sources.why_it_matters: Flawed tests and contamination reinforce conservative evaluation discipline.

sources.title: SWE-Adept: An LLM-Based Agentic Framework for Deep Codebase Analysis and Structured Issue Resolution
sources.why_it_matters: Recent agent methodology emphasizes codebase navigation, context management, checkpoints, and test-driven iteration.

sources.title: RepoAudit: An Autonomous LLM-Agent for Repository-Level Code Auditing
sources.why_it_matters: Repository audit agents need memory and validation to reduce hallucinations and false positives.

sources.title: pytest node ID documentation via Context7 / pytest docs
sources.why_it_matters: Focused node IDs are the documented way to run small reproducible checks.

sources.title: Typer testing documentation via Context7 / Typer docs
sources.why_it_matters: CliRunner tests should assert exit codes and stable output fragments.

verification.command: git diff --exit-code -- reports\calibration_seed\index.json scripts\clone_target_at_sha.py action.yml src\oida_code
python -m ruff check src/ tests/ scripts/evaluate_shadow_formula.py scripts/real_repo_shadow_smoke.py
